results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE PURPOSE THIS STUDY IS TO DESIGN AND ENGINEER AN IN VITRO 3D CELL CULTURE INTEGRATED INTO A FLOW-CELL BIOREACTOR TO CONDUCT THE TOXICOLOGICAL ASSESSMENT OF ENVIRONMENTAL POLYCYCLIC AROMATIC HYDROCARBON (PAH) MIXTURES THAT CAN BE FOUND IN FOOD. ACTIVITIES:THE RECIPIENT WILL USE HUMAN, MICE, AND PIG INTESTINAL CELLS TO ANALYZE TOXICOLOGICAL MARKERS AND PATHWAYS OF PAH MIXTURES. THIS WILL ALLOW RESEARCHERS TO EVALUATE THE ABILITY OF THE GUT MICROBIOME TO BIODEGRADE PAHS AND WHETHER THE BIODEGRADATION BY-PRODUCTS CAN CAUSE TOXIC EFFECTS TO THE INTESTINAL TRACT. SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL REPORTS, AS WELL AS A NOVEL, VALIDATED 3D CELL CULTURE THAT CAN BE INTEGRATED WITH IN VITRO AND IN VIVO RESULTS TO IDENTIFY TOXICOLOGICAL MARKERS AND PATHWAYS OF POLYCYCLIC AROMATIC HYDROCARBON (PAH) MIXTURES IN HUMANS. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROGRAM INCLUDE PEOPLE WHO ARE EXPOSED TO PAHS VIA FOOD; HUMAN HEALTH, THE ENVIRONMENT, AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC.
